methodology: Refactored to Empowered-style OKRs: a small set of company objectives, linked source objectives, source-level key results, explicit team ownership, and limited use of High Integrity Commitments.
description: Preparation Objectives for the next quarter.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe meaningful business and customer outcomes, not feature delivery.
Key results measure source objectives, while source objectives map upward into a small set of company objectives.
Every key result names executing and supporting teams so accountability is explicit.
High Integrity Commitments are used sparingly and only for work that is materially deadline-sensitive.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Make execution reliably repeatable
companyObjectives.statement: Improve the operating system behind the product so launches, service recovery, reliability, and cross-functional delivery become predictably repeatable.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Keeps teams accountable for operational outcomes and avoids turning roadmap delivery into a checklist of feature shipments.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Turn product progress into economic leverage
companyObjectives.statement: Translate better customer and operational outcomes into stronger revenue quality, retention, and strategic leverage for the business.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Separates commercial results from activity metrics so leaders can evaluate whether product work is compounding into business value.

objectives.title: 2026-Q2 Scale employer rollout without scaling admin cost
objectives.objective: Prepare the next rollout wave so multi-country employers can launch faster, with fewer payroll and policy exceptions, while preserving a clean renewal and expansion story.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Time from contract signature to employee enrollment readiness.
Share of invited employees who complete enrollment and order.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Margin after dealer, claims, and servicing cost for an active rider contract.

keyResults.target: Make launch playbooks reusable across markets
keyResults.baseline: New-country launches still depend on manual policy and payroll work
keyResults.statement: Reduce program go-live lead time by templating employer policy setup, payroll mapping, and implementation checklists.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce program go-live lead time by templating employer policy setup, payroll mapping, and implementation checklists via Employer Policy & Program Management and Quote, Subsidy & Savings Configurator.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for program go-live lead time, aligned with its focus on own the HRIS, payroll, SSO, and employer-system integration layer required for repeatable launches via Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync and Enterprise Integration Layer.

Support the KR by contributing capability for program go-live lead time, aligned with its focus on own settlement, invoicing, payroll reconciliation, and financial control across employers, riders, and partners via Settlement, Invoicing & Payroll Reconciliation and Lease Origination & Credit Decisioning.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Make launch playbooks reusable across markets
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Program go-live lead time moves far enough to show clear momentum toward make launch playbooks reusable across markets, driven by reduce program go-live lead time by templating employer policy setup, payroll mapping, and implementation checklists.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Make launch playbooks reusable across markets
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Program go-live lead time ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with make launch playbooks reusable across markets holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Improve the share of invited employees who complete enrollment
keyResults.baseline: Employees drop when the value proposition is not clear or next steps are delayed
keyResults.statement: Increase enrollment conversion rate through better communications, clearer savings presentation, and fewer order blockers.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve enrollment conversion rate through better communications, clearer savings presentation, and fewer order blockers via Employer Policy & Program Management and Quote, Subsidy & Savings Configurator.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for enrollment conversion rate, aligned with its focus on own the HRIS, payroll, SSO, and employer-system integration layer required for repeatable launches via Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync and Enterprise Integration Layer.

Support the KR by contributing capability for enrollment conversion rate, aligned with its focus on own settlement, invoicing, payroll reconciliation, and financial control across employers, riders, and partners via Settlement, Invoicing & Payroll Reconciliation and Lease Origination & Credit Decisioning.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Improve the share of invited employees who complete enrollment
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Enrollment conversion rate moves far enough to show clear momentum toward improve the share of invited employees who complete enrollment, driven by increase enrollment conversion rate through better communications, clearer savings presentation, and fewer order blockers.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Improve the share of invited employees who complete enrollment
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Enrollment conversion rate ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with improve the share of invited employees who complete enrollment holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.baseline: Margin is pressured by service variability, claims, and fragmented operations
keyResults.statement: Increase gross margin per active rider by reducing exception handling and standardizing service and support flows.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve gross margin per active rider by reducing exception handling and standardizing service and support flows via Settlement, Invoicing & Payroll Reconciliation and Lease Origination & Credit Decisioning.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for gross margin per active rider, aligned with its focus on own eventing, workflow, case, and communication primitives reused across launches, claims, and lifecycle operations via Eventing & Process Backbone and Workflow, Case & Exception Management.

Support the KR by contributing capability for gross margin per active rider, aligned with its focus on own the data foundation for KPI reporting, unit economics, and operational decision-making via Operational Data, KPI & Analytics Platform.

Support the KR by contributing capability for gross margin per active rider, aligned with its focus on own the HRIS, payroll, SSO, and employer-system integration layer required for repeatable launches via Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync and Enterprise Integration Layer.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Gross margin per active rider moves far enough to show clear momentum toward protect unit economics while scaling, driven by increase gross margin per active rider by reducing exception handling and standardizing service and support flows.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Gross margin per active rider ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with protect unit economics while scaling holding across the broader operating model.

linkedInitiatives.description: Employer launch acceleration initiative focused on improving employer policy & program management, eligibility, payroll & HRIS sync, and settlement, invoicing & payroll reconciliation to make multi-country employer rollouts faster and less manual across Employer Admin Portal, Employer Integration API, and Employer KPI Cockpit.

linkedReleases.description: Release of employer rollout improvements covering employer policy & program management, eligibility, payroll & HRIS sync, and settlement, invoicing & payroll reconciliation across Employer Admin Portal, Employer Integration API, and Employer KPI Cockpit.

objectives.title: 2026-Q2 Make the rider experience reliable after the bike is delivered
objectives.objective: Prepare the next quarter around rider trust: fewer support surprises, faster issue recovery, and stronger commuting habit formation after the initial handover.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Average number of days per month a rider uses the bike for commuting or regular utility travel.
Average time to restore rider mobility after a reported issue.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Share of contracts that renew, upgrade, or convert into a next-term bike.

keyResults.target: Help riders use the bike more often after onboarding
keyResults.baseline: Usage drops when service confidence is weak or commuting habits do not form early
keyResults.statement: Increase active commuting days per rider by improving the first-month experience, reminders, and service readiness.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve active commuting days per rider by improving the first-month experience, reminders, and service readiness via Rider Identity, Wallet & App Experience.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for active commuting days per rider, aligned with its focus on own notifications, lifecycle state visibility, renewal readiness, and contract confidence after the initial order via Communications & Notifications and Contract Lifecycle Management.

Support the KR by contributing capability for active commuting days per rider, aligned with its focus on own maintenance, repair, and service workflows that determine rider uptime and partner throughput via Service Case & Maintenance Orchestration.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Help riders use the bike more often after onboarding
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Active commuting days per rider moves far enough to show clear momentum toward help riders use the bike more often after onboarding, driven by increase active commuting days per rider by improving the first-month experience, reminders, and service readiness.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Help riders use the bike more often after onboarding
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Active commuting days per rider ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with help riders use the bike more often after onboarding holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Shorten the downtime riders experience after incidents
keyResults.baseline: Recovery depends on dealer capacity and claims coordination
keyResults.statement: Reduce incident recovery time by tightening case triage, partner routing, and rider-facing updates.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce incident recovery time by tightening case triage, partner routing, and rider-facing updates via Communications & Notifications and Contract Lifecycle Management.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for incident recovery time, aligned with its focus on own maintenance, repair, and service workflows that determine rider uptime and partner throughput via Service Case & Maintenance Orchestration.

Support the KR by contributing capability for incident recovery time, aligned with its focus on own native rider mobile experiences and the app surfaces that maintain confidence after checkout via Rider Identity, Wallet & App Experience.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Shorten the downtime riders experience after incidents
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Incident recovery time moves far enough to show clear momentum toward shorten the downtime riders experience after incidents, driven by reduce incident recovery time by tightening case triage, partner routing, and rider-facing updates.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Shorten the downtime riders experience after incidents
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Incident recovery time ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with shorten the downtime riders experience after incidents holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Build the base for lifecycle value rather than one-off acquisition
keyResults.baseline: Renewal economics are still emerging in newer markets
keyResults.statement: Increase renewal and upgrade readiness by making contract status, service history, and next-bike options easier to understand.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve renewal and upgrade readiness by making contract status, service history, and next-bike options easier to understand via Communications & Notifications and Contract Lifecycle Management.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for renewal and upgrade rate, aligned with its focus on own maintenance, repair, and service workflows that determine rider uptime and partner throughput via Service Case & Maintenance Orchestration.

Support the KR by contributing capability for renewal and upgrade rate, aligned with its focus on own native rider mobile experiences and the app surfaces that maintain confidence after checkout via Rider Identity, Wallet & App Experience.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Build the base for lifecycle value rather than one-off acquisition
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Renewal and upgrade rate moves far enough to show clear momentum toward build the base for lifecycle value rather than one-off acquisition, driven by increase renewal and upgrade readiness by making contract status, service history, and next-bike options easier to understand.

target2.target: Build the base for lifecycle value rather than one-off acquisition
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Renewal and upgrade rate ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with build the base for lifecycle value rather than one-off acquisition holding across the broader operating model.

linkedInitiatives.description: Rider conversion and trust initiative focused on improving bike catalog & recommendation, order orchestration & checkout, rider identity, wallet & app experience, and communications & notifications across iOS App, Android App, Rider Web Experience, and Lifecycle Notifications.

Fleet mobility pilot initiative focused on improving pool bike & fleet management, trip, telematics & asset monitoring, service case & maintenance orchestration, and operational data, KPI & analytics platform across Fleet Admin Console and Fleet User Mobile Experience.

linkedReleases.description: Release of rider conversion and transparency improvements covering bike catalog & recommendation, order orchestration & checkout, rider identity, wallet & app experience, and communications & notifications across iOS App, Android App, Rider Web Experience, and Lifecycle Notifications.

Release of fleet pilot improvements covering pool bike & fleet management, trip, telematics & asset monitoring, service case & maintenance orchestration, and operational data, KPI & analytics platform across Fleet Admin Console and Fleet User Mobile Experience.
